title: Yvette Lakin Named 2025–2026 Teacher of the Year
summary: Yvette is dedicated to empowering students with knowledge, confidence, and the belief that their circumstances do not define their potential.
full_text:
Palmetto Unified School District proudly recognizes Yvette Lakin as our 2025–2026 Teacher of the Year!

For Yvette, this honor is more than a title, it is a reflection of her unwavering commitment to the students she serves and their unique journeys to success. Her path to education began in an unexpected place, as a correctional officer. There, she discovered a passion for teaching and a calling to empower individuals through learning. This realization became her lifelong mission, to ensure that every student knows their potential is not defined by their circumstances.

Throughout her career with the South Carolina Department of Corrections and Palmetto Unified School District, Yvette has specialized in GED instruction for Reading Language Arts and Math. She has transformed countless lives, guiding students from uncertainty to confidence, and equipping them with the skills for lifelong achievement.

Her teaching philosophy is built on resourcefulness, humility, and empathy. Yvette meets students where they are, personalizing lessons with real-world applications, hands-on learning, and steadfast encouragement. She leads by example, modeling resilience, setting high expectations, and fostering hope in every classroom interaction.

As she puts it, “Education is not just what I do, it is who I am.”

Congratulations, Yvette, and thank you for inspiring us all.

title: Now Hiring: Secondary Math, Science & English Teachers
District Board Meeting Scheduled for July 12
State Superintendent Weaver Visits PUSD
full_text:
A Meaningful Visit to PUSD

PUSD was proud to welcome State Superintendent Weaver to the District Office for a meaningful and encouraging visit. As a district operating within a correctional environment, we value every opportunity to highlight the unique work our educators and staff do each day to support students, foster growth, and create pathways to success.

Superintendent Weaver's visit provided a valuable moment to share those efforts directly. During her time with us, she engaged in thoughtful conversation about the programs, instructional approaches, and support systems that help our students thrive in a highly structured setting. She showed genuine interest in the challenges and opportunities that come with providing education in a correctional environment and expressed strong support for our mission and the dedication of our team.

We appreciated the chance to showcase the commitment, innovation, and resilience that define PUSD. Hosting Superintendent Weaver was an honor, and we look forward to continued collaboration as we work to expand opportunities and strengthen outcomes for every student we serve.

title: Welcoming Our Newest Board of Trustee Members
summary: PUSD is thrilled to welcome Dr. Stephanie Madison and Mr. Robert Grant to the Board of Trustees.
full_text:
Palmetto Unified School District is thrilled to welcome our two newest Board of Trustee members, Dr. Stephanie Madison and Mr. Robert Grant!

Their leadership, experience, and commitment to education will be invaluable as we continue strengthening our programs and expanding opportunities for the South Carolina Department of Corrections population.

We are excited for the energy and perspective they bring to the board and look forward to the meaningful work we will accomplish together.

title: PUSD Kicks Off the 2025–2026 School Year with Inspiring Convocation and Board Meeting
summary: On July 11, 2025, PUSD launched the new academic year with a powerful Convocation and Board Meeting at the SCDC pavilion in Columbia, SC.
full_text:
On July 11, 2025, Palmetto Unified School District (PUSD) launched the new academic year with a powerful Convocation and Board Meeting at the SCDC pavilion in Columbia, SC. The day brought together educators, administrators, and district leaders to align on goals, celebrate excellence, and prepare for the year ahead.

The morning began with warm greetings from Dr. Cynthia Cash-Greene, followed by the introduction of staff across all regions by PUSD principals. It was an energizing start that set the tone for a day of connection, recognition, and forward focus.

The Board of Trustees convened at 10:00 a.m., opening with remarks from Mr. Joel Anderson, Interim Director of SCDC. Key district updates were presented by:

  • Ms. Martin and Dr. Cash-Greene – Budget and Financial Planning
  • Mr. Zimmerman – Vision, Mission, 2025-26 District Strategic Plan Goals
  • Dr. Holiday – Adult Education, GED, and WIN Attainments and Updates
  • Dr. Thompson – Career and Technical Education Report
  • Dr. Cash-Greene – Post-Secondary Program Overview

A major highlight of the day was the celebration of outstanding team members. PUSD proudly recognized:

  • The 2025–2026 Teacher of the Year: Ms. Yvette Lakin
  • The 2025–2026 Classified Employee of the Year: Mr. Donta Brown
  • Regional Teacher of the Year
  • Regional Classified Employee of the Year
  • Honored retirees, celebrated for their years of dedicated service

Following a midday break and lunch, the convocation resumed with an overview of expected outcomes for the new school year, followed by projected goals for 2025–2026. The day concluded with school recognitions and a Regional Celebration, where each region presented its own unique theme, reflecting the creativity, collaboration, and community spirit that define PUSD.

We thank all staff, board members, and guests for making this event a meaningful launch to the school year!

title: Celebrating Career and Technical Education Month: Highlighting Our Dynamic CTE Program
summary: February is CTE Month — a perfect time to spotlight the incredible hands-on programs PUSD offers across carpentry, welding, HVAC, culinary arts, and more.
full_text:
February is Career and Technical Education (CTE) Month, and there’s no better time to spotlight the incredible opportunities our CTE program offers. Through hands-on learning and practical experience, students gain the skills they need to succeed in high-demand industries, setting the foundation for rewarding careers.

Our CTE program encompasses a wide array of career pathways, ensuring that students can explore and develop their passions while preparing for the workforce.

Carpentry

Students learn to measure, cut, and construct with precision, mastering techniques to build structures that stand the test of time. Carpentry skills foster problem-solving and teamwork alongside core construction expertise.

Masonry

Craftsmanship takes center stage in masonry as students learn to shape and lay bricks, stone, and concrete — constructing durable and aesthetically pleasing structures that define our communities.

Master Hair Care

For students with a passion for creativity and personal expression, our Master Hair Care program provides the tools to excel in cosmetology, with training in styling, cutting, and coloring.

Welding

Students in our welding program gain the technical expertise to work in industries ranging from manufacturing to aerospace, contributing to the infrastructure that drives progress.

Auto Body Repair

Combining craftsmanship with cutting-edge technology, our Auto Body Repair program prepares students to restore vehicles to their former glory — from dent repair to custom paint jobs.

Auto Mechanics

Our Auto Mechanics program offers in-depth training in diagnostics, repair, and maintenance, giving students the skills to keep the world moving, one engine at a time.

Plumbing

Plumbing is essential for modern living. Our program ensures students are well-versed in installing and maintaining water systems, offering stability and real-world problem-solving daily.

Electrical

With a focus on safety and innovation, our Electrical program trains students to design and maintain electrical systems that power homes, businesses, and the technologies of tomorrow.

Small Engine Repair

Small engines power a variety of machines, from lawnmowers to recreational vehicles. Our program teaches students to diagnose and repair these engines, opening doors to a specialized and in-demand trade.

HVAC

Our HVAC program trains students to install, repair, and maintain heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems that ensure optimal indoor environments year-round.

Upholstery

Students develop craftsmanship in restoring, designing, and creating furniture pieces — learning fabric selection, padding techniques, and frame construction valuable in interior design and automotive upholstery.

Culinary Arts

For those with a passion for cooking, our Culinary Arts program provides hands-on experience in food preparation, presentation, and kitchen management, preparing students for careers in restaurants, catering, and hospitality.

Our CTE program goes beyond the classroom, emphasizing real-world application and industry connections. Students have access to internships, certifications, and mentorship opportunities, ensuring they graduate workforce-ready.

title: Jolly Times at Palmetto Unified: A Festive Holiday Gathering 2023!
summary: Our festive gathering brought together teachers and administration for a memorable celebration filled with laughter, cheer, and a dash of Christmas magic.
full_text:
Step into a winter wonderland at Palmetto Unified School District, where holiday joy was in full swing! Our festive gathering brought together teachers and administration for a memorable celebration filled with laughter, cheer, and a dash of Christmas magic.

Santa’s visit brought smiles as bright as Rudolph’s nose! Our holiday feast was a feast-ive delight, with sugar plum cookies and candy cane cocoa that delighted taste buds. The cafeteria became a gingerbread dreamland, turning the evening into a gastronomic journey through the 12 days of Christmas!

The talent show stole the spotlight, showcasing staff members’ festive spirit through song, dance, and even some elfish acrobatics. Applause rang out as loud as Christmas morning wake-up calls, proving that Palmetto Unified’s staff are the true stars of the show!

We added an extra dash of holiday hilarity with an Ugly Sweater Contest and a sing-along of classic Christmas tunes that had everyone rocking around the Christmas tree.

As the festivities wrapped up, laughter echoed through the halls, and twinkling lights sparkled a little brighter. Palmetto Unified’s staff wishes you a holly, jolly Christmas and a Happy New Year, filled with the warmth of shared moments and the magic of the season.

title: Teacher of the Year 2022–2023 — Ryan Bartola
summary: We are proud to introduce our 2022-2023 Teacher of the Year, Ryan Bartola.
full_text:
We are proud to introduce our 2022–2023 Teacher of the Year, Ryan Bartola.

During the previous school year, I was nominated for the Teacher of the Year by fellow staff members. Not knowing where this would go, I was chosen to hold the position as Teacher of the Year for the 2022–2023 School Year. This is an honor to behold.

As the Teacher of the Year, I want to represent Palmetto Unified School District’s Mission and Vision in my day-to-day duties as a school leader and teacher. I feel part of this is to become more knowledgeable of changes in education that impact our student population and help other teachers who want to improve professionally. Representing Palmetto Unified School District as Teacher of the Year is an honor that I hope to uphold for this 2022–2023 School Year.
